q: Audibility means the ability to be heard or capability of detecting sounds in certain range of ___.
For humans with normal hearing, the lower limit is $20 \text{ Hz}$ and the upper limit is about ___.
By using ___ generator, the audible frequency range can be determined.
Low frequency sounds which are under the lower limit of audibility ($20 \text{ Hz}$) are called ___.
High frequency sounds which are above the upper limit of audibility ($20\ 000 \text{ Hz}$) are called ___.
Bats and whales use ultrasound in echolocation and most ___ technologies use ultrasound.
Manufacturers of concrete use ultrasound to check for cracks or ___.
A prenatal ultrasound test uses high-frequency sound waves, inaudible to the human ear, that are transmitted through the abdomen via a device called a ___.
Ultrasonic scalers use ultrasound to remove calculus (plaque) deposits from the ___.
The transmission of high energy ultrasound may result in the certain of ___ bubbles.
Cavitation bubbles are created at sites of ___.
The sonar (sound navigation and ranging) is a type of technology that works based on ___.
Infrasound waves have long wavelengths, and are thus able to travel over ___ distances.
